# UC-19.1.31 · Fabric Interconnect HA Cluster State

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Reliability &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch the two boss brains on the fabric pair. Either can lead, but they must pick one primary so the whole control side agrees. If both claim to lead or neither will, we stop and tell you first — before the next service change can turn that disagreement into a longer outage for everyone on the domain.*

---

## Description

Correlates `cisco:ucs:inventory` `classId` rows for `topSystem`, per-FI `mgmtIf` on `sys/switch-A|B/mgmt/…` with `role` primary/subordinate, and supporting `commSvcEp` / `mgmtCntrlr` evidence with `cisco:ucs:syslog` lines that parse `code` in the E4196-class family (tune per UCSM) plus optional `cisco:ucs:faults` `faultInst` text for cluster/failover. Derives `cluster_health` from `topSystem.mode=cluster` presence, both FI mgmtIf rows, exactly one primary and one subordinate, split-brain and no-leader labels, a burst counter from syslog elections, and an optional recent_cluster_fault_hint. Control-plane Fabric Interconnect HA, not `fabricEthLanPc` (UC-19.1.5).

## Value

A broken Fabric Interconnect high-availability pair is a class-of-service event for the whole Cisco UCS domain, not a single-host problem: the two management planes must agree on which FI is primary so the cluster vIP, service profile, and API surfaces stay consistent. Split-brain risk (two primary views) and no-leader risk (neither FI primary) can block profile and firmware work, keep change windows frozen for hours while two teams prove which side of the domain is the safe one, and in the worst case produce asymmetry between IOM, chassis, and northbound management even when server-vNIC LAGs still look up. Catching the drift in inventory and syslog is the fastest way to shorten the P1: one evidence trail for platform and data-centre NOC, not three disconnected stories.

## Implementation

Install `Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs`, enable inventory and (optional) faults inputs, forward UCSM syslog to `cisco:ucs:syslog`, paste the `spl` and tune the E419 filters against five real syslog events, run every 5–10 minutes, page when `cluster_health` is not `intact` with throttle on the UCSM FQDN.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- **Product scope vs UC-19.1.5 (read this first):** If you need the Northbound Ethernet uplink LAG and fabricEthLanPc health search that scopes `^fabric/lan/(A|B)/pc-`, that is UC-19.1.5 (data plane to Nexus). This use case is only the Fabric Interconnect management-plane: Cisco UCS Manager cluster identity from topSystem, per-FI leadership from the mgmtIf managed object, optional commSvcEp and mgmtCntrlr for extra posture, and UCSM syslog in the E419 code family. Do not use cisco:ucs:stats fabricEthLanPc results here to "prove" cluster leadership; the MO trees differ by design. If the Northbound LAG is red in 19.1.5 and this control-plane check is green, you still have a data-plane ticket—bridge with the NOC, do not close the P1 on UCSM alone.

- **Educational note on a fictional sourcetype:** Some internal wikis or old runbooks still say `cisco:ucs:fi_stats` for FI high availability. That is a documentation bug. The Splunk add-on for Cisco UCS does not ship that name on the emit list. For FI HA pair posture, use `cisco:ucs:inventory` (topSystem, mgmtIf, and when enabled commSvcEp and mgmtCntrlr), `cisco:ucs:syslog` (E4196xxx–class transitions—sample to confirm the exact prefix in your build), and optionally `cisco:ucs:faults` (cluster- or failover-worded `faultInst`). Do not put `cisco:ucs:fi_stats` in your saved spl—you will get empty search results. Mentioning the old name in documentation as a warning is fine; ingesting the wrong name is not.

- **topSystem MO and mgmtIf MO (not `object_type=fi_cluster`):** Cisco UCSM does not expose a single "fi_cluster" classId. The topSystem object carries cluster vIP in `address` and, where present, `mode` to distinguish cluster from stand-alone. The mgmtIf DNs for classic dual-FI domains appear under `sys/switch-a/mgmt/...` and `sys/switch-b/mgmt/...` after you lower the dn string in SPL. The `role` field on those mgmtIf rows is the operational primary versus subordinate pair you compare in this UC. The commSvcEp object and mgmtCntrlr class add optional validation when communication or controller faults ride alongside a leadership event.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
(1) Install `Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs` (Splunkbase 2731) on a Heavy Forwarder that may run the modular UCSM HTTPS poll and on Search Heads for extractions, then open Settings → Data inputs in Splunk Web and enable the add-on’s Cisco UCS inventory-style modular input (exact stanza and UI labels depend on the 4.3.x+ build) so you receive `cisco:ucs:inventory` for at least `classId=topSystem` and `classId=mgmtIf`, and enable the optional `commSvcEp` and `mgmtCntrlr` object classes in the same form when you need extra control-plane context.

(2) Copy a stanza from `default/inputs.conf` into `local/inputs.conf`; never invent a `[script://]` for UCSM—this path is an HTTPS poll. A fragment that is safe to treat as a template (replace names and keys with what your add-on file actually ships) looks like the following. Validate every line with `splunkd` and the add-on’s own documentation before you paste into production.

```ini
# Example: copy a real cisco_ucs:// stanza from Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs default/inputs.conf
# then set disabled=0, host to your UCSM vIP or FQDN, index, interval, and enable MOs in UI
```

(3) Route UCSM syslog to your universal forwarder and normalise the sourcetype to `cisco:ucs:syslog` in props on the receiver. If you are not using `cisco:ucs:faults` yet, remove the `sourcetype="cisco:ucs:faults"` OR branch in the `appendcols` part of the spl field to avoid a noisy empty leg.

(4) After two full poll cycles, `index=cisco_ucs | stats count by sourcetype` on the collection stack must list `cisco:ucs:inventory` at a non-zero count. For syslog, `| table _time, code, host, _raw` for five `cisco:ucs:syslog` events and harden the `E4196` and `E419%` `like` filters in the spl to match the codes you actually see after each UCSM upgrade or bundle installation.

**Step 2 — Create the search, save it, and schedule the alert**
(1) The spl field in this use case is intentionally split: the main `stats` runs on `cisco:ucs:inventory` only, then `appendcols` runs a time-bounded `search` of `cisco:ucs:syslog` and optional `cisco:ucs:faults` in the same lookback (default 4h in the example) to avoid `latest()` being pulled from a trailing syslog line that does not have `classId=mgmtIf` fields. If your org forbids `appendcols`, precompute a five-minute `summary` or summary index of syslog election counts and `join` on a static key. The logic—both FI mgmtIf rows present, exactly one `primary` and one `subordinate`, `topSystem.mode=cluster` where available, a burst of election syslog in the lookback, and optional cluster-flavoured fault text—stays the same in either form.

(2) Example structured syslog the TA should be able to map into `code` and message-like fields (synthetic; your `_raw` and field names will differ slightly):

```
_time=2025-10-10T10:00:00.000+00:00
code=E4196xxx
message=…cluster…primary…subordinate…
host=ucsm-syslog-ingress.example.com
```

If your deployment extracts `message_id` instead of `code`, add it to a `coalesce` in a local copy of the `appendcols` subsearch.

(3) Schedule every five to ten minutes, alert when `cluster_health` is not `intact` or `severity_bucket` is `warning` and your CAB rules say to page, and use throttle on the UCSM service FQDN and a small hash of the A/B `role` pair so a single long ISSU window does not file duplicate incidents.

**Step 3 — Validate in UCSM and in parallel with UC-19.1.5**
(1) In the Cisco UCSM GUI, on Equipment → Fabric Interconnects (or the menu path your version documents), read the A/B high-availability roles and match them to `fi_a_role` and `fi_b_role` from the saved spl for the same vIP. If the GUI and the poll disagree, the problem is more often `rbac` on the read-only user or a partial poll during maintenance than a bug in the Cisco MO tree. Compare `_time` to UCSM local time in the GUI footer before you RMA a Fabric Interconnect on Splunk output alone.

(2) Open the 19.1.5 LAG view and check Nexus `ifOperStatus` and `fabricEthLanPc` health for the same site and clock window. A green 19.1.5 LAG and a red 19.1.31 `cluster_health` is valid: management-plane and data-plane are different MO and syslog surfaces. A red 19.1.5 and a green 19.1.31 is also possible when uplinks fail but the FIs have not yet re-elected. Never merge the two P1s into a single "network is down" story without a bridge owner for each.

(3) `cluster_mode` not equal to `cluster` can be a governed stand-alone posture during RMA, TAC, or a DR exercise; treat the alert as a true governance signal about control-plane state, not automatically as a false alarm because servers still boot.

**Step 4 — Operationalise**
(1) Build the dashboard panels in `visualization`: a status grid of FI-A and FI-B roles, a timechart of election-related syslog from a side search that uses the same `E419` filters, a red single value for "non-intact" `cluster_health` in 24h, a table of `cluster_mode`, `fi_*_present`, `fi_*_role`, `recent_election_count`, and `cluster_health`, and, if you keep the fault subsearch, a thin timechart of `recent_cluster_fault_hint` for TAC.

(2) Pager bodies should pass `cluster_mode`, the two roles, the election count, a deep link to the Cisco fault reference when `recent_cluster_fault_hint` is non-zero, and a short one-line reminder that 19.1.5 covers Northbound LAG, not 19.1.31, so the Nexus NOC does not waste a bridge slot when the LAGs are still green but UCSM is in `mode_standalone` by design.

(3) Retain 90 days of saved-search result artifacts for P1s that touch the cluster: auditors and post-mortem teams will ask for a chain from inventory to syslog to the exact Cisco change record on the FIs, not a screen grab from a single pane alone.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**
- No `cisco:ucs:inventory` in the index: the modular UCSM HTTPS input is off, 401/403 to the vIP, TLS trust store missing the issuing CA, or the `index` in `inputs.local` is not the index your role can search. Start in `_internal` and `splunkd.log` for the Splunk TA’s own python and SSL error lines before you tune SPL.

- Roles stick at `absent` but the GUI shows a subordinate on fabric B: your Distinguished Name for mgmtIf is not the `^sys/switch-b/mgmt/` path this search assumes in your build. `| head 50` the raw `dn` for `classId=mgmtIf` in your lab, widen the `match` with TAC and Cisco U CSM documentation for your release, and record the new regex in change management before you edit production.

- `appendcols` returns all-zero counters but a raw `cisco:ucs:syslog` search shows E419 text in 4h: the subsearch is hitting a stricter time bound than your panel, or the NOC role cannot read the `cisco_ucs` index for the subsearch only (rare, but it happens with saved-search owner context in 9.x and 10.x if you are not consistent across schedule and ad-hoc).

- A brief `split_brain` label in Splunk right after a Cisco N-in-1 FI firmware change may clear as soon as the next poll and the TAC-supervised election completes. If the CAB ticket, Cisco maintenance window, and TAC are aligned, set a 120 second alert delay, not a permanent mute, so you can still see a no-leader state if the activation stalls.

- NTP skew above two minutes between UCSM, the Heavy Forwarder, and the indexer can make `_time` ordering look like leadership drift. Fix NTP and KV-store _indextime plots for that cluster before a hardware RMA is justified on timing alone. Evidence integrity is still a Sev1 class problem even when the FIs are healthy on the bench.

- Cisco Intersight-primary domains (see the exclusions field) may stop classic UCSM inventory polls for the same FIs. A sudden, sustained drop in `cisco_ucs` volume for a cluster is often a data-source or ownership change, not proof that a physical HA pair is healthy in UCSM terms. Point observability to the new pipeline instead of re-tuning a dead 19.1.31 search forever.

**Hardware note (FI families):** UCS 6324 and UCS 6332 Fabric Interconnects often serve compact and branch pods; UCS 6454 and UCS 6536 FIs are common in large A/B data-centre pairs. The spl in this use case is independent of line rate. It only depends on a supported Cisco UCSM build that still exposes the topSystem and mgmtIf DNs the poll requests.

**Close-out checklist (before you mark production-ready):** **(1)** `index` and `sourcetype` probes in Search return all three or two streams you plan to use. **(2)** `classId` and `code` `fieldsummary` in the same hour as a TAC-observed test election. **(3)** RFO template updated so P1s reference both 19.1.5 and 19.1.31 rows when Nexus and UCSM are both involved, with owners named. **(4)** CAB calendar rows for RMA, firmware, and vPC maintenance that are known to create brief `absent` mgmtIf or burst E419 lines. **(5)** Runbook with Settings → Data inputs screenshots for the modular UCSM form so the next SRE is not reverse-engineering your inputs.conf from memory.

Additional operational depth (non-exhaustive): if your organisation also indexes Cisco Nexus syslog for the same vPC, build a post-incident “triangulation” view that joins 19.1.31 table output with 19.1.5’s `parent_pc` in the same 15 minutes using host-level links or change-ticket IDs, not a blind union of unrelated sourcetypes. If you are required to use CIM or a tstats-only architecture, re-implement the count-only part of the appendcols in a summary index or data model acceleration for cisco:ucs:syslog; this JSON deliberately omits `cimModels` and `cimSpl` because the canonical vendor feed is not CIM-tagged. If your security team must redact the management vIP, store a lookup from cluster-id to vIP and drive both UCs on cluster-id in tokens instead of the raw address in the alert text.


## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:inventory" earliest=-4h
| search (classId="topSystem" OR classId="mgmtIf" OR classId="commSvcEp" OR classId="mgmtCntrlr")
| eval ldn=lower(coalesce(dn,""))
| eval cidl=lower(coalesce(classId, class_id, ""))
| eval mode_t=if(cidl="topsystem", lower(trim(coalesce(mode, sys_mode, ""))), null())
| eval is_fi_a_mgmt=if(cidl="mgmtif" AND match(ldn, "^sys/switch-a/mgmt/"), 1, 0)
| eval is_fi_b_mgmt=if(cidl="mgmtif" AND match(ldn, "^sys/switch-b/mgmt/"), 1, 0)
| eval role_t=if(cidl="mgmtif", lower(trim(coalesce(role, mgmt_role, fi_role, ""))), null())
| sort 0 - _time
| stats
    latest(eval(if(cidl="topsystem", mode_t, null()))) as cluster_mode
    latest(eval(if(is_fi_a_mgmt=1, role_t, null()))) as fi_a_role
    latest(eval(if(is_fi_b_mgmt=1, role_t, null()))) as fi_b_role
    max(is_fi_a_mgmt) as fi_a_present
    max(is_fi_b_mgmt) as fi_b_present
| appendcols maxout=1 [
    search index=cisco_ucs (
        (sourcetype="cisco:ucs:syslog" (like(code, "E4196%") OR like(code, "E419%") OR match(_raw, "(?i)election|primary now|subordinate now|cluster lead|split.?brain")))
        OR (sourcetype="cisco:ucs:faults" AND match(lower(coalesce(cause, descr, _raw, "")), "clust|failover|inter[- ]?connect|split|ha|mgmt|election"))
      ) earliest=-4h
    | eval is_election=if(sourcetype="cisco:ucs:syslog" AND (like(code, "E4196%") OR like(code, "E419%") OR match(_raw, "(?i)election|primary now|subordinate now")), 1, 0)
    | eval is_clust_flt=if(sourcetype="cisco:ucs:faults" AND (like(lower(cause), "%clust%") OR like(lower(cause), "%fail%") OR like(lower(descr), "%inter%") OR like(lower(descr), "%split%") OR like(lower(descr), "%subordinate%") OR like(lower(descr), "%primary%")), 1, 0)
    | stats sum(is_election) as recent_election_count, sum(is_clust_flt) as recent_cluster_fault_hint
  ]
| fillnull value=0 recent_election_count recent_cluster_fault_hint
| eval fi_a_role=if(isnull(fi_a_role) OR fi_a_role="", "absent", fi_a_role)
| eval fi_b_role=if(isnull(fi_b_role) OR fi_b_role="", "absent", fi_b_role)
| eval cluster_health=case(
    cluster_mode!="cluster" AND !isnull(cluster_mode) AND cluster_mode!="", "mode_standalone",
    fi_a_present=0 AND fi_b_present=0, "no_leader_no_data",
    fi_a_present=0 OR fi_b_present=0, "single_fi_only",
    fi_a_role="primary" AND fi_b_role="primary", "split_brain",
    fi_a_role="subordinate" AND fi_b_role="subordinate", "no_leader",
    (fi_a_role="primary" AND fi_b_role="subordinate") OR (fi_a_role="subordinate" AND fi_b_role="primary"), if(recent_election_count>3, "leadership_unstable", "intact"),
    1=1, "leadership_drift"
  )
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    cluster_health IN ("split_brain", "no_leader", "no_leader_no_data"), "critical",
    cluster_health IN ("single_fi_only", "leadership_unstable", "leadership_drift", "mode_standalone") OR recent_cluster_fault_hint>0, "warning",
    cluster_health="intact", "ok",
    1=1, "warning"
  )
| table cluster_mode, fi_a_present, fi_b_present, fi_a_role, fi_b_role, recent_election_count, recent_cluster_fault_hint, cluster_health, severity_bucket
```

## Visualization

(1) Status grid or paired single values for FI-A and FI-B `mgmtIf` role. (2) Timechart of election counts from a companion syslog search over 7d. (3) Single value: non-`intact` `cluster_health` in 24h. (4) Table: `cluster_mode`, presence, roles, `recent_election_count`, `cluster_health`, `severity_bucket`. (5) Optional timechart of `recent_cluster_fault_hint` from `cisco:ucs:faults`.

## Known False Positives

UCSM ISSU or Fabric Interconnect N-in-1 firmware activation (expected E419-style syslog burst while roles settle). Planned subordinate FI reseat or RMA (one mgmtIf missing for multiple polls; reconcile with the hardware ticket). Scheduled L1/L2 or DR test between FI and Nexus that also moves the cluster vIP or a temporary stand-alone mode under change control. Controller certificate or TLS profile rotation (one FI missing from inventory for minutes while the other is healthy; treat as cert/NTP, not a split-brain, until the poll path is clean). Major UCSM upgrade where the syslog `code` prefix changes — tighten the `like` against fresh samples, not a three-year runbook. Third-party syslog forwarders that case-fold or duplicate lines, changing apparent election counts. Read-only org policies that strip equipment-class syslog severities in non-prod, producing zero election rows while prod still has data.
